title: CRDs, Controllers, Operators, Admission Policies, Webhooks, and Extension Trust · Kubernetes Operations Room
summary: A platform pairing session predicts each API transition before producing the first working trusted extension API and controller contract for crds, controllers, operators, admission policies, webhooks, and extension trust. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of trusted extension API and controller contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design CRD names, scope, versions, structural OpenAPI schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion.
Build or evaluate controllers from watches, work queues, level-based reconciliation, ownership, idempotency, finalizers, status, retries, leader election, and rate limits.
Choose built-in admission, ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, validating webhook, or mutating webhook from expressiveness, failure policy, ordering, timeout, availability, and audit needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design crd names, scope, versions, structural openapi schemas, defaulting, validation, pruning, status subresource, scale, storage version, and conversion. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion, keep K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A workshop CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Kubernetes Architecture, Objects, and Cluster Administration — Controls control-plane and node components, API and etcd boundaries, reconciliation, object identity and status, namespaces, ownership, cluster lifecycle, conformance, and recovery.

hints: For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall.

For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A CRD automatically supplies a controller and preserves unknown fields and old versions forever.

options.text: State the K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

A CRD automatically supplies a controller and preserves unknown fields and old versions forever. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion.

For commit to the mission model, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof. This does not satisfy K8s Crd Schema Status Conversion for trusted extension API and controller contract.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Extension Upgrade Trust
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-extension-upgrade-trust-1lxl4j". Tailor its extension-contract.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Extension Upgrade Trust.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Extension Upgrade Trust into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Extension Upgrade Trust, keep K8s Extension Upgrade Trust connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-extension-upgrade-trust-1lxl4j
  - competency: k8s-extension-upgrade-trust
    artifact: extension-contract.yaml
    decision: extension upgrade trust
    environment: clean conformance cluster with only required add-ons installed
    fault-injection: remove DNS egress from an otherwise ready workload
    retained-proof: object UID generation observedGeneration and status conditions
    case-key: 1lxl4j
    change: |
      crd: structural-schema-with-status
      storage-version: v1
      conversion: tested
      controller: idempotent-rate-limited-reconcile
      finalizer: external-state-owned
      admission: validating-policy-first
      webhook: bounded-fail-policy
      uninstall: dependency-ordered
    verify: kubectl get crd,validatingadmissionpolicy,validatingwebhookconfiguration -o json
    failure: Reject schema-free CRDs, event-script controllers, unbounded webhooks, fail-open-by-default, or operator deletion as external-state cleanup.
    repair: Version and validate the API, test conversion, bound reconciliation and admission, preserve status, contain failure, and rehearse upgrade and uninstall.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The K8s Extension Upgrade Trust structural check is only one gate. trusted extension API and controller contract must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Competency probe: Evaluate extension provenance, permissions, webhooks, CRDs, conversion, compatibility, backup, canary, observability, failure containment, and uninstall safety.
